items.title: These 'readers' created in remote NT are unlike those in most schools

children.content: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

gallery.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

thumbnail.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

items.title: Crucial exhibits unveiled to jury in Irma Palasics cold case trial

children.content: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

image.altText: A fridge with many containers in it.

items.title: Low branch reported two weeks before cyclist's death, coronial reveals

children.content: A cyclist’s death on a popular Canberra path could have been prevented if the ACT government had acted promptly on a public warning about a dangerous overhanging branch, coronial findings reveal.

image.altText: A cyclist riding on an outdoor path.

items.title: Public hearing into hiring of new CIT CEO cancelled as probe continues

children.content: The chair of the CIT board told an ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry the complaints made against chief executive Margot McNeill related to perceptions of conflicts of interest around employments and contracts.

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

articles.title: The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf

children.content: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

articles.title: How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

children.content: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil, like the Philippines in Southeast Asia, are most vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: Arnhem Land rock art raises questions about mainland Tasmanian tigers

children.content: Newly documented rock art in Arnhem Land has raised questions about how long Tasmanian tigers lived on the mainland before they were wiped out.

items.title: Study says economic value of grey-headed flying fox poo worth millions

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

children.content: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon

children.content: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list
